questions.question: In a `watchEffect` with async code, which reactive values ARE tracked?
options: All reactive values accessed anywhere in the function
Only reactive values accessed after the last `await`
Only reactive values accessed synchronously before the first `await`
No reactive values are tracked in async watchEffect

questions.question: Why does Vue stop tracking dependencies after an `await` in `watchEffect`?
options: It's a design limitation that will be fixed in a future Vue version
`await` yields back to the event loop and Vue can't guarantee which microtask runs next
The Promise executor runs in a different reactive scope
Async functions return a Promise, not a value, so tracking is impossible

questions.question: What is the safer alternative to `watchEffect` for async watchers with multiple dependencies?
options: Using `watch` with explicit sources declared upfront
Reading all values before calling the async function
